title: What are databases?
summary: Basic structure of RDBMS: tables, columns, rows, rules of relations, and introduction of ERD.
content_html:
What are databases?

A structured set of data that represents a model of reality. Basic operations: CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete).

Relational Database structure

TerminologyMeaning
Column (Attribute/Field)Save a specific type of information
Row (Record)Represents a single entity
Table (Relation)Collection of rows and columns

Rules of Relations

  • Cells contain single values (atomic)
  • Each column stores one type of information
  • Column names are unique
  • Order is not important
  • Rows are unique

Why is good design needed?

  • Duplicate data: causes slowness, difficulty maintaining, leading to anomalies
  • Inconsistent data: spelling error, different format
  • Calculated fields: contains values from other fields → conflict when updating
  • Multivalue fields: violates 1NF

ERD (Entity Relationship Diagram)

Contains ingredients: Entity type, Entity, Attribute, Relationship. Used to model data structures at the conceptual design stage.
